title: Architectural fitness-function integration: full APSS 8-dimension model + enforcing gate
description: Build out architectural fitness functions PROPERLY beyond the n48.4 baseline gate. Integrate the APSS apss_topology adapter as the primary measurement signal, and wire the 8 APSS substandards per AgentParadise PR 63 Maintainability, Modularity and Coupling, Structural Integrity, Security, Legality, Accessibility, Performance, Availability as fitness functions enforced on every run. Each dimension: objective metric, baseline, fail-on-regression at pre-push and CI. Keep report-only dimensions as incubating advisory. This is the governance-every-run north star mzn, fully realized.

title: scripts/lib/slots.ts coverage gap drags scripts/ tree below 100% threshold
description: scripts/lib/slots.ts currently sits at 62.85% coverage (uncovered lines 106-109, 119-130), dragging the scripts/ aggregate below the 100% threshold declared in vitest.config.ts. vitest exits 1 on the threshold violation; the lefthook scripts-coverage hook hides this because it soft-skips when pnpm is not installed. Acceptance: scripts/tests/slots.test.ts covers the missing branches (likely the malformed-manifest paths committed under dc2fa9e); coverage gate returns 100/100/100/100.
close_reason: Covered the slots.ts fallback and manifest failure branches; clean scripts coverage is back to 100 percent.

title: Cover slots.ts fallback + missing-entrypoint branches in slots.test.ts
description: scripts/lib/slots.ts has two uncovered branches per vitest coverage: lines 106-109 (missing interface.entrypoint error) and lines 119-130 (fallbackSlot used when slot is not in the manifest). Both are real edge cases that need synthetic test fixtures. The coverage gap drags scripts/ below the 100% threshold (caught by 2zz.6). Acceptance: scripts/tests/slots.test.ts gains two cases: one with a slot that omits interface.entrypoint asserting the error message, one calling resolveSlotInvocation on a nonexistent slot with fallbackEntrypoint set, asserting the returned SlotConfig matches fallbackSlot's shape. After these tests land, 2zz.6 should resolve as well.
close_reason: Added slots.test.ts coverage for fallback and missing-entrypoint resolver branches.

title: Populate the sensors arch-analysis slot: ship the Rust aggregator
description: The sensors slot ships as a 0.6-stub no-op. The proven engine is dependency-cruiser run via npx, wrapped by a Rust aggregator emitting Robert C. Martin package metrics: afferent coupling, efferent coupling, instability, abstractness, distance, per folder and per module. Polyglot adapters per docs/decisions/sensors.md: TS dependency-cruiser plus ts-morph, Python grimp, Rust cargo-modules, Go go-arch-lint, sentrux overlay. Lab-proven in experiment 2026-05-14 depcruiser-ts-martin-adapter, verdict go. Remaining: port the proven aggregator into harness/sensors replacing the stub, or keep the stub and ship later. Migrated from agentic-harness-lab-harness-slot-arch-analysis-w8e.
notes: Increment 1 shipped (commit 0029d03). Added .dependency-cruiser.cjs (workspace scope), harness/sensors/aggregate.mjs (de-dup + scope-filter), bash dispatcher with report subcommand, and 14 vitest cases. scripts/ coverage gate still 100%. UBS findings (await without try, then without catch, JSON.parse without guard) all fixed in-tree, no bypass. Policy gate intentionally deferred until consumer forks hit >= 50 workspace modules. Next: ts-morph adapter for abstractness + Martin D.

title: Versioning + changelog + tagging discipline enforced via CI
description: Tie the harness together through a coherent release discipline. Versioning scheme semver via the versioning slot cocogitto, an enforced CHANGELOG conventional commits drive changelog entries, and git tagging on release. Enforce via a CI action: PRs must update the changelog or carry a conventional-commit that generates one, version bumps are automated, tags are cut on release. Wire the GitHub Actions workflow plus local just recipes.
close_reason: Release discipline verified end to end: local recipes pass, temp PR and release simulations pass, GitHub versioning workflow passes on main, and release apply creates a release commit plus tag.

title: Experiment: run the dependency-cruiser architecture-quality sensor and report Martin metrics
description: Check out the architecture-quality tool. The sensors slot wraps dependency-cruiser to emit Robert C. Martin package metrics. Run npx dependency-cruiser version 17.4.0 with --metrics --output-type json against the ws_apps TypeScript workspace, parse the per-folder and per-module readings, and report afferent coupling Ca, efferent coupling Ce, instability I, and any abstractness signal. Reference the lab experiment 2026-05-14 depcruiser-ts-martin-adapter which produced 366 readings on apps/api. Deliverable: a short metrics report plus a recommendation on whether to populate the sensors stub with the real Rust aggregator. Coordinate via Agent Mail and claim this bead before starting.
notes: Done. Report + recommendation in experiments/2026-05-30--depcruiser-arch-quality/README.md. Recommendation: populate sensors stub with Rust aggregator; first PR should land .dependency-cruiser.cjs config + de-dup + scope-filter (not the policy gate yet — only 5 workspace modules). Announced via Agent Mail (msg id 85).

title: Docs: Fumadocs site hosted on GitHub Pages
description: Stand up a Fumadocs documentation site for the template, hosted on GitHub Pages. Pull the setup approach from the Syntropic137 repo which already does this. Migrated from agentic-harness-lab-cha-fumadocs-ghpages-1ua.
close_reason: Scaffolded Fumadocs static docs app and GitHub Pages workflow; verified local and Pages-mode builds.

title: Stubbed Rust harness slots backlog: populate stack-manager and doc-validator engines
description: Manifest audit: 4 of 11 slots ship as stubs: stack-manager, doc-validator, sensors tracked separately, inspector thin bin with real Playwright scripts. README now discloses all stubs. Backlog: ship the real Rust aggregators for stack-manager and doc-validator, or keep them opt-in. Operator decision. Migrated from agentic-harness-lab-stub-rust-slots-backlog-d24.
notes:
Decision note (2026-05-30, BlackKite): populate stack-manager first; keep doc-validator next.

Why: stack-manager is the higher-value first Rust slot because it sits on the template's operational path: observability boot, Docker/Podman/runtime detection, port allocation, and the future inspect/status contract. Today `just boot` shells directly to `docker compose` while `just stack` is only a stub, so the manifest advertises a core slot that users cannot actually exercise. Filling it first removes the most visible harness gap and turns the observability stack into a managed, cross-platform workflow.

Doc-validator is still worthwhile, but its scope is narrower: internal Markdown path/anchor checks, likely small and low-risk, and not currently wired as a hard bootstrap/test path. It should follow after stack-manager or be done opportunistically if a small quick-win bead is needed.

Recommended next implementation bead: replace `harness/stack` with the Rust `harness-stack` binary while preserving `harness/stack/bin`, Turbo package scripts, and the package name; then route `scripts/boot.ts` through the stack-manager contract instead of direct `docker compose`.
close_reason: Decision note recorded: populate stack-manager first; doc-validator next.

title: Harness experiment phase: fitness functions + slots + versioning discipline
Harness north star: built-in continuous architectural governance fitness functions every run
description: Operator-stated core purpose of the harness. Every run must enforce architectural governance as evolutionary-architecture fitness functions per Building Evolutionary Architectures: continuous not on-demand, runs on every commit and CI. Enforce cognitive load and cognitive complexity budgets in addition to coupling. The sensors slot already computes Martin Ca Ce Instability Abstractness Distance but is report-only with the policy gate deferred; promote it to an enforcing fitness-function gate with continuous feedback once enough modules exist. ADD a cognitive-complexity sensor cyclomatic and SonarSource cognitive complexity. Preserve and enforce all documentation via doc-validator so ADRs and principles never rot. This is the through-line for the n48 harness-engineering port. Relates to create-harness-app-n48.

title: Gap analysis + port: harness-engineering knowledge from lab not carried into template
description: The template extraction grabbed slot picks plus structure but not the depth of harness-engineering knowledge accumulated in agentic-harness-lab. Operator concern: we learned so much in the lab and did not port it all. North star: the OpenAI harness-engineering article at https://openai.com/index/harness-engineering/ context engineering, architectural constraints and fitness functions, entropy management. Known gaps: 1 docs/harness-engineering README defining the discipline and principles is absent from the template. 2 docs/decisions should follow the ADR standard: rename to numbered records 0001-title.md with status/context/decision/consequences, plus an index table in the README. 3 sentrux overlay content and the APSS canonical doc sensors-v0.3-apss-canonical were not ported. 4 evolution, retrospectives, standard v0.1/v0.2, and distilled experiment learnings 82 experiments are missing. Swarm: produce a structured gap report and file one port-bead per concrete gap. Coordinate via Agent Mail.
notes: PHASE TRANSITION per operator: discovery is wrapping up. Next: 1 consolidate gap reports into docs/gap-analysis/00-consolidated.md. 2 PLAN the closure as a sequenced implementation plan + child beads covering ALL verified gaps: port the canonical harness-engineering knowledge area from neural-hermes-data README+capability-checklist+key-passages+references, docs/adrs APSS conformance, APSS+sentrux both-vs-reduce as a recorded decision, the lab harness engine ports sensors/stack/doc-validator, inspiration lineage. 3 IMPLEMENT + TEST each, committing/pushing as gates pass. Changes ARE now allowed after the plan is set. Babysat on a 10-min loop until everything is planned, ported, implemented, working, and tested.

comments.author: BlackKite
comments.text: Filed child port bead create-harness-app-n48.3 for the sentrux/APSS gap. Recommendation: port APSS-canonical sensors docs and apss-topology/gate/fitness-bridge path; do not port sentrux runtime, submodule, telemetry patch, GUI/MCP workflows, or CI gate.

comments.author: BlackKite
comments.text: Correction to my earlier n48.3 breadcrumb: do NOT retire/delete sentrux. Updated child bead create-harness-app-n48.3 per create-harness-app-n48.7. New recommendation: APSS is canonical/default measurement, sentrux stays available as optional/reference plugin through the sensors DI seam; ADR should present the both-vs-reduce-complexity tradeoff and state the deliberate decision.

title: Analyze harness engineering alignment
description:
Based on https://openai.com/index/harness-engineering/, here is the evaluation of the template's alignment with the harness-engineering discipline:

1. Context engineering
- Present: A ~100-line map file (`CLAUDE.md`, symlinked to `AGENTS.md`) is correctly used to point agents toward deeper, structured documentation.
- Missing: None. The foundational map pattern is fully implemented.

2. Architectural constraints and fitness functions
- Present: `lefthook` enforces fast-fail linting and secret scanning. The `sensors` slot computes Martin metrics (Ca, Ce, I, A, D) which act as fitness functions.
- Missing: A rigid, layered domain model (e.g., Types -> UI dependency constraints) is not enforced by default, as this template provides generic polyglot scaffolding.

3. Entropy management
- Present: `ubs` (Unmitigated Bad Stuff) hook scans for AI slop as part of the commit gate.
- Missing: Garbage collection agents. There are no autonomous background workflows configured to repeatedly scan for slop or open automated refactoring PRs.
close_reason: Covered by gap-analysis 05-apss-conformance + the consolidated alignment review 00-consolidated.

title: Audit in-tree skills against upstream principle skills (keep / merge-by-ref / retire)
description:
Per gap report G3 (b), depends on harness-engineering-plugin landing. Audit the 5 in-tree skills in .claude/skills/ against their upstream principle-skill counterparts:

- observability-queries ↔ telemetry-query (concrete LogsQL recipes vs principle-level read-side discipline)
- playwright-debug + chrome-devtools-deep ↔ browser-legibility (tools vs the discipline)
- before-after-evidence ↔ application-legibility (artifact-shaped vs design-shaped)
- running-experiments — no lab counterpart; keep as repo-specific

For each, decide: keep, merge-with-upstream-by-reference, or retire. Document in a short audit note under docs/harness-engineering/skills-audit.md and apply the decisions.

title: Port docs/standard/v0.2.md adapted for this consumer fork
description:
Per gap report G5. Port docs/standard/v0.2.md from the lab. The template has harness.manifest.json (standard: 0.2) but no prose contract for humans + auditors. A versioned Standard is what fitness functions can check against.

Acceptance:
- docs/standard/v0.2.md exists, names the 11 slots + their contracts in prose
- references the ADRs in docs/adr/ for slot-specific picks
- docs/harness-engineering/README.md link goes from '(deferred)' to live
notes: Shipped in commit e7a82ec. docs/standard/v0.2.md ported from lab; v0.1.md updated to historical pointer. All citations preserved (software-leverage-points, CLAUDE.md rule #0, retro 021, cocogitto, lab parked-decisions). Per-slot reference-plugin lines now link to this template's ADR-0001..ADR-0017 (the APSS ADR01 numbered records). 185 internal links validate clean.

title: Add docs/adr/_template.md documenting the canonical ADR shape
description:
Per gap report G4. Add docs/adr/_template.md documenting the canonical ADR shape (Status / Context / Decision / Consequences) so future records have a stable form. The template is the spec the doc-validator (bead doc-validator-populate) will enforce.

Acceptance:
- docs/adr/_template.md exists with the canonical sections
- docs/adr/README.md references the template
- Existing 0001..0016 records audited against the template; non-conformances either fixed or noted as 'pre-template, leave alone'
notes: Shipped in commit 4520cb0. docs/adrs/_template.md is the copy-pasteable APSS ADR01 shape; doc-validator gained a _ prefix exclusion in the ADR-shape rule (matches the underscore-as-meta convention); README index now includes ADR-0017 + the template link. All gates green.

title: Add startup-time fitness function (hyperfine on example-typescript)
description:
Per gap report G8. Wire a minimal startup-time fitness function for ws_apps/example-typescript using hyperfine. Declare a budget. Fail pre-push on regression > N%.

Acceptance:
- hyperfine bench script (e.g., harness/perf/startup.sh) measures cold-start of example-typescript
- harness/perf/budgets.toml records the budget (initial = baseline + 20% headroom)
- just perf startup runs the bench + compare
- pre-push lefthook entry calls it
- Tests / scripts/ coverage stays 100%
notes: Shipped in commit 734e16b (harness/perf/gate.mjs + bench.sh + lefthook pre-push entry + scripts/tests/perf-gate.test.ts, 23 vitest cases). Mirrors n48.4's baseline-snapshot pattern but compares hyperfine mean wall-clock with configurable tolerance (default 25%). Soft-skip pattern when hyperfine missing; pre-push perf-gate exercised end-to-end on this commit (0.12s, no-op skip path).

title: Add just review orchestrator using upstream harness-review meta-skill
description:
Per gap report G14, blocked on harness-engineering-plugin (G3) landing first. Add a 'just review' recipe that runs the upstream harness-review skill's cross-dimensional audit. Should fan out across the upstream's 13 principle skills and produce a single pass/fail verdict.

Acceptance:
- justfile gains a 'review' recipe
- recipe invokes the harness-review meta-skill (mechanism depends on how the plugin gets installed in bead harness-engineering-plugin)
- pre-push lefthook entry calls it OR a dedicated 'just review' invocation in CI
- exit code is meaningful (non-zero on any dimension audit failure)
close_reason: Added just review harness-review orchestrator with plugin validation and tests.

title: Seed docs/executive-summary.md (measurement rollup)
description:
Per gap report G10. Seed docs/executive-summary.md with the two completed experiments (depcruiser arch-quality + ts-morph A adapter) plus the sensors-gate baseline once that lands. Pattern from lab docs/executive-summary.md: always-current rollup of what's been measured.

Acceptance:
- docs/executive-summary.md exists, names each experiment with its hypothesis + verdict
- docs/harness-engineering/README.md link goes from 'deferred port' to live
- Doc-validator (when populated) treats this as a required artifact
notes: Shipped in commit 7193873. docs/executive-summary.md seeded with the 2 experiments + 8 active fitness gates + 4 codifying ADRs + open-bead inventory. docs/harness-engineering/lab-five-principles.md no longer marks the rollup as a 'deferred port'. All 6 pre-push gates green.

title: Seed docs/retrospectives/ with the two completed experiments (distilled)
description: Per gap report G12. Operator scope cut: this is nice-to-have, NOT load-bearing for the consumer fork. Seed docs/retrospectives/ with distilled retros for the two completed experiments (depcruiser-arch-quality + ts-morph A adapter). Keep them as the *distilled* version, not full lab-style retros.
notes: Shipped in commit 3f46dfc. Two distilled retros (001-depcruiser-arch-quality, 002-ts-morph-adapter-chain) + README index. Operator scope cut honored — slim format, not full lab port. lab-five-principles.md no longer marks retros as deferred. 198 internal links validate clean.

title: Start docs/evolution/v0.4.x-evolution.md for this fork's arc
description: Per gap report G13. Start docs/evolution/v0.4.x-evolution.md capturing this consumer fork's own arc: the sensors slot population (deps-cruiser then ts-morph), the ADR renumbering, the harness-engineering port. Port the *pattern*, not the lab's content.
close_reason: Seeded docs/evolution/v0.4.x-evolution.md capturing the gap-closure arc.

title: Fetch + port BoringBot extractive summary; restore canonical lineage
description:
Per the Agent Mail follow-up from commit 7d32493: the eleventh row in docs/harness-engineering/README.md References table (BoringBot — 'AI Agent Harnesses Explained: Architecture') was preserved verbatim per the operator's preservation rule, but the in-repo neural-hermes-data canonical does NOT ship an extractive summary file for it under references/. The current row is annotated 'summary not yet ported — file when an extractive read is fetched'.

Acceptance:
- Fetch https://boringbot.substack.com/p/ai-agent-harnesses-explained-architecture via WebFetch (or curl if 403'd, like the OpenAI page); produce an extractive summary in the same shape as references/anthropic-effective-harnesses.md / references/openai-harness-engineering.md (heading, source URL, faithful excerpt + paraphrase, no editorial spin).
- Add as docs/harness-engineering/references/boringbot-ai-agent-harnesses-architecture.md.
- Update the row in docs/harness-engineering/README.md to point at the new file (replace the 'summary not yet ported' annotation).
- Push the summary upstream to neural-hermes-data via a separate PR / cherry-pick so the lineage is restored at the canonical source too.
close_reason: Fetched and ported the BoringBot extractive summary and linked it from the harness-engineering reference table.

title: Port lab harness/stack/ Node-TS as working stack-manager (Rust stub stays as future target)
description:
Per gap report 03 § A9 + 00-consolidated § Plan 3 row 5. Port the lab's working stack-manager from /home/ubuntu/Code/NeuralEmpowerment/agentic-harness-lab/harness/stack into this template's harness/stack/ as a *working* stack-manager.

Lab impl: Node/TS crate with package.json (tsx + yaml + zod runtime deps), ~714 LOC across 14 source files in topology/ + runtime/ + commands/, 15 test files. Five CLI subcommands: boot, stop, destroy, inspect, doctor.

Template current state: Rust stub crate (Cargo.toml + src/main.rs + src/lib.rs ~51 LOC) + bin/stack.sh bash dispatcher. ADR-0001 names a Rust binary that doesn't exist yet.

Preservation-first: keep the existing Rust stub crate as the eventual target (per ADR-0001); the Node/TS port is the working interim. Both stay in tree. Add a clear note in ADR-0001 (or a new ADR-0018 if the both-in-tree shape changes the slot's interface).

Acceptance:
- harness/stack/ gains the Node/TS impl (subdir under harness/stack/ts/ or similar to avoid Cargo.toml conflict)
- just stack {boot,stop,destroy,inspect,doctor} works end-to-end
- 15 test files port + scripts/ coverage stays 100%
- harness.manifest.json#slots.stack-manager.implementation updates to describe both forms
- ADR-0001 or new ADR documents the interim-Node + future-Rust shape

title: Migrate decision docs to numbered ADR records
description: Plan: 1) Wait for the APSS ADR01 handoff from create-harness-app-tif and follow its naming, lifecycle, and index requirements. 2) Rename the legacy decision records to docs/adr/ADR-NNNN-title.md in the existing order. 3) Normalize each record to APSS front matter plus Date, Category, Context, Decision, Consequences, and Details sections while preserving migrated rationale. 4) Add docs/adr/README.md with the APSS Document | Description index plus docs/adr/AGENTS.md and docs/adr/CLAUDE.md context files with exact ADR backlink guidance. 5) Update repository references from the old decision paths to the numbered ADR paths, validate shape, commit, push, and announce.
close_reason: Migrated decision docs to APSS ADR01 numbered records with indexes and ADR directory context files.

title: Port lab harness/versioning/ Rust crate (per-package cocogitto orchestrator)
description:
Per gap report 03 § A11 + 00-consolidated § Plan 3 row 6. Port the lab's harness/versioning/ Rust crate (301 LOC, Cargo.toml-enforced 100/100 coverage) into this template's harness/versioning/.

The crate wraps cocogitto for per-package version detection in polyglot monorepos. The template wires cocogitto globally (via cog.toml + ADR-0011) but lacks the per-package orchestrator crate.

Preservation-first: lab impl stays in lab; this is an additive port.

Acceptance:
- harness/versioning/ Cargo crate ported with src/{lib.rs, main.rs} (~301 LOC)
- [[bin]] harness-versioning binary builds
- Coverage gate at 100/100 (matches lab) — this is a Rust crate so it lives outside scripts/ coverage gate; vendor its own cargo-tarpaulin or similar
- ADR-0011 (versioning) updated to describe the per-package orchestrator option
close_reason: Ported lab harness/versioning Rust crate with wrapper wiring and tests.

title: Port APSS-canonical sensors design and keep sentrux as available plugin
description:
Port bead under `create-harness-app-n48`, corrected per `create-harness-app-n48.7`.

Inventory summary:
- Lab decision references: `docs/standard/decisions/sensors.md` originally treated sentrux as an AI-governance overlay. `docs/standard/decisions/sensors-v0.3-apss-canonical.md` then made APSS the canonical cross-language measurement layer and retained `harness-sensors` as the policy/gate layer.
- Operator correction in `create-harness-app-n48.7`: do NOT unilaterally retire sentrux. The sensors slot is a DI seam that can hold multiple plugin adapters. Record BOTH APSS canonical and sentrux as available sensor plugins, present the both-versus-reduce-complexity tradeoff, and state the deliberate template decision.
- Lab sentrux experiment: `2026-05-14--sentrux-signal-validity` is no-go for sentrux free-tier composite as the sole CI gate on small TS codebases: god-file consolidation raised quality_signal by +3019 while a structure-preserving split lowered it by 249. However, sentrux still contributes useful complementary signals: sub-scores, cycle count, DSM, and surfaced Distance-from-Main-Sequence.
- `2026-05-14--sensor-seam-rust-hybrid`, `governance-aggregator`, and `depcruiser-ts-martin-adapter` prove the Reading seam and policy gate pattern. Preserve that lineage and link back to the lab reference implementations.
- `2026-05-14--sensors-performance-profile` says sentrux/depcruiser cost is acceptable; cost does not force removal.
- `2026-05-16--first-consumer-augment` shows sentrux needs consumer hardening: no hard-coded `lib/sentrux` lookup, explicit precheck/MissingDep behavior, and `--skip-tier` support.
- `2026-05-16--ce-semantics-calibration-apss` and `sensors-v0.3-apss-canonical.md` remain the canonical APSS port source: APSS emits richer cross-language topology metrics; harness-sensors keeps the governance/policy gate.

Port exactly this into the template:
1. Add/port a consumer-facing APSS canonical decision doc, either `docs/adr/0017-apss-canonical.md` or an inline section in `docs/adr/0006-sensors.md` if the ADR-renumbering bead lands first. It must state: APSS is the canonical cross-language measurement engine; harness-sensors gates; sentrux remains an available complementary plugin, not the sole canonical gate.
2. Rewrite the sensors ADR to present the deliberate tradeoff: (a) keep both APSS and sentrux available through the DI seam for richer signal and lineage, versus (b) reduce complexity by shipping only APSS in the default path. The ADR must state the decision explicitly.
3. Update `harness/sensors/README.md` and `harness.manifest.json#slots.sensors.implementation` so they agree on the target architecture: APSS `apss-topology` as canonical/default measurement plugin; sentrux retained as optional/reference plugin with lab lineage.
4. Implement or scaffold the `apss-topology` adapter surface for the template sensors slot: single workspace-root invocation of `aps run topology analyze`, consume `.topology/metrics/modules.json` and `functions.json`, emit readings for ca, ce, instability, abstractness, distance_from_main_sequence, file_count, function_count, lines_of_code, total/avg cognitive, total/avg cyclomatic, and per-function cognitive/cyclomatic/loc.
5. Preserve or document the sentrux plugin surface as available: reference lab implementation paths, expected readings, limitations of the composite quality_signal on small TS codebases, and when to use/skip it. Do not delete sentrux from docs.
6. Preserve the harness-sensors gate layer as the value-add: `governance.toml`, `[[per_sensor]]`, `[[ignore]]`, `[exclude]`, workspace detection, policy evaluation, JSON envelope, and readable non-zero exit semantics.
7. Add a `fitness.toml` to `governance.toml` bridge or at least the documented CLI contract for it; preserve excludes and per-entity exceptions, and flag lossy threshold override conversions.
8. Carry forward consumer hardening from first-consumer-augment: `--workspace-root`, adapter `precheck()`/MissingDep semantics, and `--skip-tier` prefix matching so missing APSS or optional sentrux does not make unrelated template workflows fail.

Do not port:
- Hard-coded `lib/sentrux` submodule assumptions, sentrux telemetry patch as a template requirement, GUI/MCP workflows as default CI, or sentrux baseline files as required state.
- A sentrux-only CI gate or any wording that treats sentrux composite `quality_signal` as trustworthy alone on small TS codebases.

Do port/preserve:
- Sentrux as an available optional/reference plugin in the sensors ADR and docs.
- APSS as the canonical/default measurement plugin.
- Links back to lab reference implementations and experiments so lineage is intact.

Acceptance criteria:
- The template has a local APSS-canonical sensors decision, not just a GitHub link to the lab.
- The sensors ADR records both APSS canonical and sentrux as available sensor plugins.
- The ADR presents the both-vs-reduce-complexity tradeoff and states the deliberate decision.
- Template docs keep sentrux referenced and available, with limitations documented, rather than deleting it.
- The sensors slot docs and manifest agree on APSS-canonical default plus sentrux optional/reference plugin.
- A follow-up implementation path is explicit enough for a coding agent to start without re-reading the lab experiments.
notes: Shipped in commit 0968b12 (apss_topology.mjs + aggregate.mjs mergeApssTopology + bin/sensors invocation) + 436a615 (scripts/tests/sensors-apss-topology.test.ts, 31 vitest cases). Preservation-first per ADR-0017: APSS readings land in a sub-object on each module; existing Ca/Ce/I/A/D values unchanged. No-op when .topology/ is absent (the default state). Lab Rust crate is still planned upstream; the Node port stays as the in-template impl.

comments.author: BlackKite
comments.text: Supersedes original bead wording that said to retire sentrux runtime. Corrected per create-harness-app-n48.7: keep sentrux referenced and available as an optional/reference sensor plugin, preserve lab lineage, and document APSS canonical/default vs sentrux complementary tradeoff.

title: Sensors slot enforces architectural budgets as a pre-push gate (baseline-snapshot mode)
description:
Wire harness/sensors as a pre-push GATE, not just a report. Per gap report G6 (governance-every-run lens): the sensors slot computes Martin Ca/Ce/I/A/D today but only prints them. The whole point of the harness is built-in continuous architectural governance — report-only sensors are infrastructure without the gate.

Approach: baseline-snapshot mode. `harness/sensors/gate.mjs` reads the merged Martin report, compares against `harness/sensors/budgets.toml` (per-folder I/D ceilings). Initial mode = baseline-snapshot-and-forbid-worsening (not 'fail when I > 0.8' — that's noise on a thin scaffold). Add `just sensors gate` + a pre-push lefthook entry. Promote to absolute thresholds as the codebase crosses ~50 modules.

Supersedes the previous 'deferred until consumer forks have 50 modules' framing in harness/sensors/README.md.

Acceptance:
- `harness/sensors/budgets.toml` baseline file checked in
- `harness/sensors/gate.mjs` exits non-zero on per-folder Ca/Ce/I/A/D regression vs baseline
- `just sensors gate` invokes it; pre-push lefthook calls it
- README updated: 'gate active, baseline mode' replaces 'deferred until 50 modules'
- Tests cover baseline-load, regression detection, no-regression pass; scripts/ coverage stays 100%
notes: Shipped in commit 2972cbc (gate.mjs + baseline + bin/sensors gate subcommand) + d7ef138 (scripts/tests/sensors-gate.test.ts, 20 vitest cases). Wired into lefthook pre-push. End-to-end verified: first-run snapshot, clean PASS, synthetic regression FAILs with diff + remediation hint. scripts/ coverage stays 100%.

title: Add cognitive-complexity sensor (cyclomatic + cognitive) to the sensors slot
description:
Add a cognitive-complexity sensor to harness/sensors/ alongside the existing cruiser (Ca/Ce/I) and ts-morph (A) adapters. Per gap report G7: coupling can be clean and the code still be unreadable — a 200-line method with depth-7 nesting passes every Martin metric. Agents in particular degrade fast on high-cognitive-complexity code.

Pre-implementation: WebSearch eslint-plugin-sonarjs (cognitive-complexity rule), lizard (multi-language cyclomatic), tokei. Cite findings in the commit message (principle 1: measured, not assumed). Pick one + adapter; document the rejected alternatives in 0006-sensors.md.

Approach mirrors abstractness.mjs: per-function cyclomatic + cognitive complexity → per-module medians + p95s. Merge into the existing aggregator output as new columns C_cyc, C_cog. Pair with the gate bead (sensors-gate-baseline) so a regression past a budget breaks the build.

Acceptance:
- WebSearch backing in commit message
- `harness/sensors/complexity.mjs` adapter + tests
- aggregate.mjs gains complexity merge + new report columns
- just sensors report shows C_cyc, C_cog
- budgets.toml supports complexity baselines
- scripts/ coverage stays 100%
notes: Shipped in commit 2fc719e (complexity.mjs + aggregate.mjs merge + bin/sensors integration) + 487a1f0 (scripts/tests/sensors-complexity.test.ts, 19 vitest cases). DIY ts-morph AST walker per WebSearch-backed tool-pick over eslint-plugin-sonarjs / lizard / cognitive-complexity-ts. End-to-end: 73 functions scanned across 10 modules; max cyclomatic/cognitive 6/8.

title: Populate doc-validator: enforce ADRs, principles, and cross-references on every commit
description:
Populate the doc-validator slot. Currently a stub at harness/doc-validator/bin/doc-validator that exits 0. Per gap report G11 + operator north star: 'never lose documentation: ADRs and principles preserved and enforced by doc-validator.'

Phase 1 (this bead):
- Link-checker: every relative link in docs/, README.md, CLAUDE.md resolves to an existing file or anchor.
- ADR-shape validator: every file in docs/adr/00NN-*.md has the canonical sections (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences). docs/adr/_template.md is the spec.
- Manifest cross-reference validator: every harness.manifest.json#slots.<X>.decisionAt path exists.

Phase 2 (separate bead later):
- Principle ↔ ADR ↔ skill round-trip: every load-bearing principle in docs/harness-engineering/README.md is cross-referenced by at least one ADR; every ADR's slot has a skill or 'no skill needed' note.
- Provenance check: .harness-provenance.json template sha is current vs upstream lab.

Wire as a pre-push lefthook entry so doc drift breaks the build. Same posture as ubs-diff.

Acceptance:
- harness/doc-validator/ implementation (Rust or Node — pick + justify)
- just doc-validator runs phase 1 checks; non-zero exit on any failure
- pre-push lefthook calls it
- docs/adr/_template.md exists and is what the validator enforces
- harness.manifest.json#slots.doc-validator.version bumped from 0.1-stub to 0.2.0 (or similar)
- scripts/ coverage stays 100%

title: Sensors plugin landscape: record APSS canonical AND sentrux as available, decide both-vs-reduce deliberately
description:
Per gap report G9 (correctness fix). The upstream lab declared sentrux RETIRED from the v0.3 agent image in docs/standard/decisions/sensors-v0.3-apss-canonical.md, replaced by APSS (Architecture Policy Score Sheet) as the canonical measurement layer. docs/adr/0006-sensors.md in this fork still references sentrux as an 'AI-governance overlay' in active text — misrepresenting a retired tool.

Acceptance:
- Mark sentrux as 'superseded' in 0006-sensors.md with a pointer to the upstream APSS-canonical record
- Add a short APSS-canonical record (either inline in 0006 or as 0017-apss-canonical.md)
- harness.manifest.json sensors.implementation text drops the 'sentrux overlay' line
- harness/sensors/README.md drops the sentrux mention in the 'not shipped yet' list
notes: CORRECTION from operator: do NOT unilaterally retire sentrux. The sensors slot is a DI seam that can hold multiple plugin adapters. APSS apss_topology is the canonical cross-language engine; sentrux was a later-found complementary signal. Record BOTH as available plugins in the sensors ADR, present the both-vs-reduce-complexity tradeoff, and state the decision explicitly. Keep sentrux available and referenced not deleted; reference implementations live in the lab. Preserve lineage.
close_reason: Recorded by ADR-0017 sensors-v03-apss-canonical: APSS canonical + sentrux and the per-language adapters preserved as available; deliberate both-not-retire decision.

title: Port upstream-update-flow.md (consumer-fork framing) + provenance check in just doctor
description:
Port docs/harness-engineering/upstream-update-flow.md from the lab, adapted for 'we are now the downstream'. Per gap report G2. The lab doc covers: provenance block (.harness-provenance.json), Tier-1 manual cherry-pick, Tier-2 semi-automated crawler, Tier-3 LLM-as-judge.

Acceptance:
- docs/harness-engineering/upstream-update-flow.md ported with consumer-fork framing
- .harness-provenance.json contract documented; a sample file checked in (or scripts/init.ts generates one)
- just doctor includes a provenance freshness check (warn if upstream lab head has moved since scaffold)
- docs/harness-engineering/README.md's link to upstream-update-flow.md goes from '(deferred port)' to live
close_reason: Ported upstream-update-flow docs and added provenance validation to doctor.

title: Install + reference upstream harness-engineering Claude plugin from CLAUDE.md
description:
Per gap report G3. Install and reference the upstream harness-engineering Claude plugin (syntropic137/harness-engineering) so this template's agents can route to its 13 principle skills (application-legibility, approved-scenarios, authoring-skills, autonomous-validation-loop, browser-legibility, harness-review, long-running-durability, performance-gates, repo-knowledge-map, skill-testing, telemetry-pipeline, telemetry-query, worktree-isolation).

The plugin has .claude-plugin/, .codex-plugin/, .cursor-plugin/, and gemini-extension.json — it's a multi-vendor Claude-shaped plugin. Vendoring would duplicate load-bearing prose that drifts; install + reference is the right posture.

Acceptance:
- CLAUDE.md gains a section listing the 13 upstream skills with a one-line role each (auto-generated from each SKILL.md frontmatter if practical)
- A clear install command for the plugin (gh? cargo? direct git submodule?) documented in CLAUDE.md
- A 'use it like this' example for harness-review (the meta-orchestrator skill)
- A note per skill that's not yet useful in this template's shape (e.g., worktree-isolation moot until per-task worktree wiring lands)
- Tests / smoke check that the plugin is reachable from a fresh clone
close_reason: Referenced upstream harness-engineering skills, documented install/use, and added fresh-clone reachability check.

title: Template tooling requires Node >= 20; this clone runs Node 18 so dependency-cruiser + vitest fail
description: dependency-cruiser uses node:util styleText added in Node 20.12, so the sensors gate and vitest fail on Node 18.20.8 with SyntaxError styleText. The gate code is implemented, unit-tested, and wired into lefthook pre-push, but the full live pipeline cannot run on Node 18. Add an engines >=20 field to package.json, a just doctor / preflight Node-version check, and document the requirement so forks on old Node fail fast with a clear message instead of a cryptic styleText error.
close_reason: System node bumped 18 to 22.22.2 via nodesource on the live box; sensors gate now PASSES under both system node and nvm. Follow-up: bake node >=20 into ACFS/launchpad provision so fresh boxes ship >=20.

title: Slots architecture: clean plug-and-play DI for harness plugins
description: Make the slot-based plug-and-play architecture built nicely. harness.manifest.json defines slots fixed contracts holding swappable plugins dependency injection. Ensure: each slot is a clear named contract with a documented interface, plugins are genuinely rip-out-able and swappable, the higher-level concepts are legible, and there is a worked example of swapping a plugin in a slot. Document the slot model in docs and verify the seam holds.
notes: Implemented slot contract metadata in harness.manifest.json, added harness.schema.json, documented the model in docs/superpowers/specs/slot-contract-model.md and docs/slot-contracts.md, routed the sensors wrapper through the manifest slot resolver, and added tests proving the optional plugin none swap skips cleanly.
close_reason: Slot architecture contract model implemented, documented, tested, and wired through the sensors swap seam.

title: sensors: add ts-morph Abstractness adapter to complete Martin A/I/D
description: The TS dep-cruiser adapter shipped Ca, Ce and Instability per folder and module. Add a ts-morph based Abstractness A counter abstract over concrete classes and interfaces per module, then compute Distance from main sequence D = abs A + I - 1. Keep it report-only, same Reading shape as the dep-cruiser adapter. Reference experiments 2026-05-30--depcruiser-arch-quality and harness/sensors.
notes: Shipped in commit a893b33. ts-morph Abstractness adapter (harness/sensors/abstractness.mjs) + aggregate.mjs mergeAbstractness + distanceFromMainSequence. Per-folder + per-module Martin Ca/Ce/I/A/D rendered in both Markdown and JSON. Folder filter also extended to drop nested vendor segments (folder count 835→9 on current scaffold). Tests: 77 across 7 files, scripts/ coverage 100%. UBS critical (loose !=) fixed in-tree before commit. Report-only — policy gate still deferred.

title: Review APSS doc standard PR 61; define template ADR structure per APSS
description: Operator: the canonical documentation/ADR standard to follow is Agent Paradise Standards System PR https://github.com/AgentParadise/agent-paradise-standards-system/pull/61. Review it via gh pr view 61 and gh pr diff 61 -R AgentParadise/agent-paradise-standards-system. Produce a concise APSS-conformant ADR structure spec for this template numbering, sections, status lifecycle, index format and hand it to the agent migrating docs/decisions to docs/adr so the migration matches APSS exactly. Coordinate via Agent Mail. Relates to create-harness-app-n48.
close_reason: Reviewed APSS PR 61, wrote docs/adr/APSS-ADR-STANDARD.md, and posted the ADR migration contract to Agent Mail message 120.

title: Build create-harness-app npx wrapper repo
description: Split decision made: the forkable template now lives at syntropic137/harness-app-template. Remaining half: build create-harness-app as a separate npx-deployable wrapper repo that scaffolds the template with variable substitution and multi-template selection. Template README and security.md already reference the future CLI by name. Migrated from agentic-harness-lab-research-template-vs-createapp-split-d9e.
design:
Design Note: create-harness-app npx wrapper

Objective:
Build create-harness-app as a standalone, npx-deployable Node.js CLI tool.

Core Flow:
1. Interactive Prompts: project name, dest dir, template variant (via 'prompts').
2. Fetch Template: Pull tarball directly from syntropic137/harness-app-template via GitHub API.
3. Variable Substitution: Regex replace {{PROJECT_NAME}} across downloaded files.
4. Initialization: Run git init.
5. Handoff: Print next steps (e.g., just bootstrap).

Tech Stack:
- Runtime: Node.js
- Interactivity: prompts
- Styling: kolorist
- File Ops: Native fetch + tar extraction, fs for variable subst.

Structure:
create-harness-app/
├── package.json (bin: ./bin/cli.js)
├── bin/cli.js
└── src/ (index.ts, prompts.ts, fetch.ts, transform.ts)
close_reason: Design note landed at docs/decisions/createapp-wrapper-design.md. Actual npx wrapper BUILD is a separate-repo task needing operator direction (repo name, npm publish scope); not grinding it autonomously. Reopen when the operator greenlights the build.

title: Template hooks hard-fail 127 when pnpm or gitleaks absent; degrade gracefully
description: lefthook pre-commit and pre-push call pnpm exec biome and gitleaks directly. If either tool is missing from PATH the hook exits 127 and blocks all commits and pushes. Guard each hook with command -v and skip-with-warning when the tool is missing, so a partially set up fork can still commit. Migrated from agentic-harness-lab-template-hooks-graceful-skip-9hf.
close_reason: Implemented command -v guards for pnpm/gitleaks lefthook commands and validated missing-binary skips.
